# anti-hall

> A Claude Code marketplace + plugin that makes Claude Code verify before it claims.
> It injects a verify-first protocol at session start (re-firing after compaction), a
> short rotating nudge every turn, mechanical guards (no AI commit self-credit, no silent
> force-push, command-delegation, anti-fork-bomb, anti-speculation, task-list discipline via
> tasklist-guard), an always-on output-presentation rule (rule K: present for scannability),
> an always-on scope & fidelity discipline (simplest sufficient solution; intent over letter;
> confirm before expanding scope; match rigor to blast radius; finish what was asked / drop
> nothing), a TTL'd user-override escape hatch (~/.anti-hall/skip.json), seven workflow skills,
> a live two-line statusline, and an opt-in mcp-reaper companion (macOS + Linux) that kills
> orphaned MCP-server processes. All hooks are pure Node.js (built-ins only) and run on macOS,
> Linux, and Windows; the one prerequisite is Node >= 18 on PATH.

anti-hall targets four failure modes of coding assistants: eagerness (acting before
investigating), hallucination (stating unverified facts as truth), fix-before-diagnosis
(fixing before proving root cause), and fake completion (claiming done without running
the check). It keeps the discipline alive across long sessions with a layered defense:
protocol injection, per-turn nudges, and mechanical hooks that cannot be argued with.

## Install

```
/plugin marketplace add talas9/anti-hall
/plugin install anti-hall@anti-hall
```

Requires Node.js >= 18 on PATH; without it the hooks silently no-op. Updates via the
`/plugin` manager; updates pull on restart if autoUpdate is enabled (or via the /plugin manager).

## Overview

- [README](README.md): overview, the four failure modes, two-layer design, statusline, AFK mode, quickstart.
- [Plugin README](plugins/anti-hall/README.md): full component reference, how-it-works, configuration, troubleshooting, contributing.
- [CHANGELOG](CHANGELOG.md): version history (current: see plugin.json).
- [AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md): prose mirror of the verify-first Iron Law for Codex / cross-tool agents (repo root; not bundled by /plugin install).
- [docs/KB-claude-codex.md](docs/KB-claude-codex.md): knowledge base on Claude Code hook contracts, prompting, and verified Claude Code internals (cmux subagent detection, macOS memory, statusline architecture).
- [docs/KB.md](docs/KB.md): plugin knowledge base index (hooks, skills, design decisions).
- [docs/CONTEXT-PRESERVATION-KB.md](docs/CONTEXT-PRESERVATION-KB.md): swarm-researched context-discipline KB (findings-externalization, compaction cadence, context-rot sweet spot).
- [docs/TASK-WORK.md](docs/TASK-WORK.md): task-list + `.anti-hall-progress.md` work-tracking discipline.
- [docs/TASKLIST-GUARD.md](docs/TASKLIST-GUARD.md): tasklist-guard usage, thresholds, and escape hatch.
- [docs/E2E-TESTING.md](docs/E2E-TESTING.md): the zero-dependency `node:test` E2E suite and how to run it.

## Hooks (plugins/anti-hall/hooks/)

All pure Node, fail-open. Registered in hooks.json.

- [verify-first-full.js](plugins/anti-hall/hooks/verify-first-full.js): SessionStart — full Iron-Law + rationalization-table protocol, the always-on scope & fidelity discipline (simplest sufficient solution; intent over letter; confirm before expanding scope; match rigor to blast radius; finish what was asked / drop nothing), and skill primer; re-fires after compaction (source=compact).
- [verify-first.js](plugins/anti-hall/hooks/verify-first.js): UserPromptSubmit — short varying one-line nudge.
- [git-guard.js](plugins/anti-hall/hooks/git-guard.js): PreToolUse Bash — quote-aware tokenizer blocks AI self-credit attribution in git commit trailers AND in `gh pr/issue/release create|edit|comment` --body/--title (🤖 footer, Co-Authored-By, claude.com/claude-code link; inline values only, --body-file fail-open), plus git push --force.
- [api-guard.js](plugins/anti-hall/hooks/api-guard.js): PreToolUse Write/Edit/MultiEdit — the mechanical answer to API hallucination: resolves module.attribute references in code-to-be-written against the installed python3/node and BLOCKS the write when a real module is missing the attribute. Default scope = stdlib/builtins (import-safe, no untrusted code); opt-in ANTIHALL_API_GUARD_THIRDPARTY=1 also verifies installed 3rd-party packages (off by default — a deadly-loop proved that verifying a package = importing it = running its code at edit time). Never probes local/relative modules; 0 FP + full in-scope catch on eval/api-guard-bench.js; fail-open.
- [command-guard.js](plugins/anti-hall/hooks/command-guard.js): PreToolUse Bash — blocks heavy commands in the COORDINATOR (pushes them to subagents); subagent-aware via the hook payload (works under cmux where the entrypoint env is unreliable).
- [swarm-guard.js](plugins/anti-hall/hooks/swarm-guard.js): PreToolUse Agent/Task — anti-fork-bomb: spawn-rate cap + real available-memory check (vm_stat / MemAvailable, not os.freemem()).
- [phase-tracker.js](plugins/anti-hall/hooks/phase-tracker.js): PreToolUse Agent/Task — records subagent spawns to a homedir log so the statusline shows live swarm activity automatically. Never blocks.
- [agent-watchdog.js](plugins/anti-hall/hooks/agent-watchdog.js): CLI heartbeat helper (NOT a hook) — scans `~/.anti-hall/agents/*.json` heartbeats and reports stale/hung subagents; run manually by the orchestration skill.
- [task-tracker.js](plugins/anti-hall/hooks/task-tracker.js): UserPromptSubmit — task-list discipline directive (+ a one-line freshness note when open/stale tasks exist).
- [task-guard.js](plugins/anti-hall/hooks/task-guard.js): Stop (loop-safe) — blocks once when the session ends with genuinely-open tasks.
- [tasklist-guard.js](plugins/anti-hall/hooks/tasklist-guard.js): Stop — enforces a live task-list + fresh `.anti-hall-progress.md` for non-trivial work (≥ threshold file-mutating actions); coexists with task-guard with its own independent block cap.
- [skip-guard.js](plugins/anti-hall/hooks/skip-guard.js): shared user-override escape-hatch primitive read by the guards — a TTL'd, granular `~/.anti-hall/skip.json` marker; a broad `all` skip excludes the destructive git-guard (it must be named explicitly).
- [speculation-guard.js](plugins/anti-hall/hooks/speculation-guard.js): Stop — flags hedge-word speculation; confident inference-as-fact is the opt-in speculation-judge (Tier 3).
- [speculation-judge.js](plugins/anti-hall/hooks/speculation-judge.js): Stop — opt-in LLM-evaluated speculation check (off by default).
- [ship-it-guard.js](plugins/anti-hall/hooks/ship-it-guard.js): PreToolUse Write/Edit/MultiEdit — OPT-IN, default OFF (the only opt-in code-edit gate). With ANTIHALL_SHIPIT_GATE ∈ {1,true,yes,on}, blocks a CODE edit on a hard-risk path (migration/auth/.github/workflows/security) when no PLAN.md exists (repo root or .planning/PLAN.md). Enforces artifact existence only, conservative, fail-open; no effect when unset.
- [graphify-session.js](plugins/anti-hall/hooks/graphify-session.js) / [graphify-reminder.js](plugins/anti-hall/hooks/graphify-reminder.js) / [graphify-guard.js](plugins/anti-hall/hooks/graphify-guard.js): query-the-graph-first priming, update reminder, and search guard (no-op without a graph).
- [hooks.json](plugins/anti-hall/hooks/hooks.json): hook registration manifest.

## Skills (plugins/anti-hall/skills/)

Invoke as /anti-hall:<name>.

- [root-cause](plugins/anti-hall/skills/root-cause/SKILL.md): evidence-driven debugging to the original + root cause (also enforced always-on as a discipline).
- [orchestration](plugins/anti-hall/skills/orchestration/SKILL.md): non-blocking swarm; coordinator/worker hierarchy; watchdog + heartbeat; live phase statusline; verify delegated work — a subagent's "done/passing" is an unverified claim re-checked against ground truth before marking complete; defaults delegated heavy/parallel work to the background (coordinator passes run_in_background so the user needn't background it manually) while still verifying each on completion (also always-on).
- [deadly-loop](plugins/anti-hall/skills/deadly-loop/SKILL.md): iterative Reviewer + Critic debate + fix-waves until zero new P0s.
- [ship-it](plugins/anti-hall/skills/ship-it/SKILL.md): one lean workflow for shipping any change correctly, scaled S/M/L to blast radius — brainstorm + plan IN PLAN MODE (ExitPlanMode is the approval gate), harden the plan with the deadly-loop BEFORE code, fan large work out as a Workflow swarm, verify each phase with fresh evidence + a vacuous-test guard until zero NEW P0s. Replaces feature-launch.
- [install-statusline](plugins/anti-hall/skills/install-statusline/SKILL.md): installs the statusLine entry on demand (global by default, per-project on request) + restart reminder.
- [doctor](plugins/anti-hall/skills/doctor/SKILL.md): health-check — confirms Node is found, every hook is present + syntax-valid, and the guards actually fire (live behavioral self-tests).
- [deadly-loop-multi](plugins/anti-hall/skills/deadly-loop-multi/SKILL.md): scaled-up deadly-loop — N parallel Reviewer + Critic pairs with diversified lenses, then deduplicate + synthesize (double / triple / quadruple).
- [MODEL-POLICY.md](plugins/anti-hall/skills/MODEL-POLICY.md): shared Reviewer/Critic roster (Opus + Codex), triplicated across skill references; keep in sync.

## Statusline (plugins/anti-hall/statusline/)

Two-line statusline the plugin renders itself.

- Line 1 (rich): project name, git (branch/worktree/stash/status/ahead-behind), model, effort, subagents, duration, context %, cost, optional GSD .planning phase.
- Line 2 (always-on, 3 tiers): coordinator-set phase progress bar > auto "orchestrating · N agents" activity bar (from phase-tracker) > idle context-window gauge.
- [STATUSLINE.md](plugins/anti-hall/statusline/STATUSLINE.md): install + revert.
- [statusline.js](plugins/anti-hall/statusline/statusline.js): dispatcher (line 1 = base wrap or own-dispatch rich renderer; line 2 = phase-bar.js).
- [statusline-rich.js](plugins/anti-hall/statusline/statusline-rich.js): the rich line-1 renderer.
- [phase-bar.js](plugins/anti-hall/statusline/phase-bar.js): the 3-tier line-2 renderer.
- [phase.js](plugins/anti-hall/statusline/phase.js): the orchestrator's progress writer (set/advance/step/agents/clear).
- [install-statusline.js](plugins/anti-hall/statusline/install-statusline.js) / [uninstall-statusline.js](plugins/anti-hall/statusline/uninstall-statusline.js): installer / uninstaller.

## Companion (plugins/anti-hall/companion/)

Opt-in, NOT a hook. macOS + Linux interval job; Windows is a documented no-op (no parent-death reparenting + PID recycling make external orphan detection unsafe — the correct fix there is Job Objects set by the spawner).

- [mcp-reaper.js](plugins/anti-hall/companion/mcp-reaper.js): kills ONLY orphaned MCP-server processes (parent already died). Safety invariant: reaps a process only if it matches a generic MCP signature AND its parent is a reaper/init (launchd / init / `systemd --user`); since Unix reparents a dead process's children, a live MCP always has a live spawner as parent, so killing an in-use server is impossible by construction. Recognizes Python MCPs too (uvx/uv + underscore mcp_server_* forms). Limitation: an MCP run as a LaunchAgent / `systemd --user` unit / OS service shares init as a parent (like a leaked orphan) and can be reaped — exclude it via ANTIHALL_REAPER_EXCLUDE='name|name'. Env: MCP_REAP_DRYRUN=1, MCP_REAP_GRACE, ANTIHALL_REAPER_MATCH, ANTIHALL_REAPER_EXCLUDE.
- [install-reaper.js](plugins/anti-hall/companion/install-reaper.js): `node install-reaper.js` installs (macOS LaunchAgent / Linux systemd --user timer, cron fallback); `--uninstall` removes; `--dry-run` previews.
- [companion/README.md](plugins/anti-hall/companion/README.md): full reference + safety rationale.

## Manifests

- [plugins/anti-hall/.claude-plugin/plugin.json](plugins/anti-hall/.claude-plugin/plugin.json): plugin manifest; version is the sole authority.
- [.claude-plugin/marketplace.json](.claude-plugin/marketplace.json): marketplace manifest.

## Tests

- [tests/](tests/): zero-dependency `node --test` (node:test) E2E suite — 329 passing (+2 platform-skipped), 331 total, covering the hooks (guards, api-guard stdlib/builtin/3rd-party checks + shadowing/portability/RCE-no-execute, git-guard gh-PR self-credit, speculation, tasklist-guard Bash-regex cases + history-ledger reminder, synthesize-never-relay nudge, etc.). Run with `node --test`. api-guard's catch/FP bench: `node eval/api-guard-bench.js`.
- [.github/workflows/test.yml](.github/workflows/test.yml): CI runs the suite on push/PR.

## Read more

Start with [README.md](README.md), then [plugins/anti-hall/README.md](plugins/anti-hall/README.md)
for depth. The design rationale and verified Claude Code internals are in
[docs/KB-claude-codex.md](docs/KB-claude-codex.md).
